what_input_feature: The IHDR color_type byte at file offset 0x19 must hold the exact value 0x03 (PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE) so that png_ptr->color_type compares equal at pngrutil.c:1973; the loser inputs instead carry 0x06 (RGBA), which fails the equality and routes to the gray/color (false-side) arms.
why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds carry 03 at offset 0x19 (W bytes 03(.)x3) and take the true branch at L1973 (W T=3 F=0), reaching the palette-index body at lines 1974-1983.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry 06 at offset 0x19 (L bytes 06(.)x19) and never take the true side at L1973 (L T=0 F=19), instead falling through to the COLOR_MASK_COLOR else arm at L1996/L2014.
mechanism_attribution: Both arms have cmplog/I2S, so I2S is held constant and cannot be the differentiator; the decisive delta is value_profile. value_profile adds the CMP_MAP gradient: the equality png_ptr->color_type == PNG_COLOR_TYPE_PALETTE is intercepted, and the Hamming/prefix-distance bucket rewards inputs whose color_type byte moves toward the constant 3, letting value_profile_cmplog discover the rarer color_type=3 PNG that plain cmplog could not.

what_input_feature: An XML element bearing an attribute literally named 'xml:id' (prefix bytes 'xml', local-name bytes 'id' followed by NUL), so the interned prefix pointer equals ctxt->str_xml and localname[0..2]=='i','d',0 at SAX2.c:2173-2175.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winner seeds contain the literal 'xml:id="' attribute token (Seed 2 bytes at 0x0e-0x15: 78 6d / 6c 3a 69 64 3d 22 = 'xm','l:id="'), exercising the xml:id true branch; the winner takes the true side T=13 F=0 at L2173 and reaches the xmlValidateNCName / xmlAddID body (lines 2181-2190 marked [W]).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds carry DTD/ATTLIST and xmlns:xlink tokens (Seed 2/4: 'ATTLIST b xmlns:xlink', 'SYSTEM "dtds') but never the 'xml:id' attribute name, so at L2173 the loser is T=0 F=39 and falls through into the xmlIsID / xmlIsRef else-arms (L2191/L2193 [L]).
mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers share the same input-to-state substitution stage, so the single-technique delta is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. value_profile_cmplog gets Hamming/prefix-distance feedback on the byte comparisons that build the 'xml:id' attribute name (the prefix-interning string match and the localname[0]=='i', [1]=='d', [2]==0 char chain), rewarding partial-match intermediate inputs and climbing toward the exact xml:id token; plain cmplog lacks this gradient and must hit the multi-byte token by substitution / havoc alone, which it rarely completes within budget.

what_input_feature: Inside a DTD ATTLIST attribute-type position, the input must present the two-character token 'I''D' (RAW=='I' AND NXT(1)=='D') after the earlier CMP5/CMP6 literal tests for 'CDATA', 'IDREFS', 'IDREF', 'ENTITY' have all failed — i.e. the token is 'ID' and not a longer ID-prefixed keyword.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and actually hits the true side (avg hitcount=6); its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose attribute-type bytes get Hamming-closer to the matched literals, letting it converge a token to exactly the 2-byte 'ID' that satisfies (RAW=='I')&&(NXT(1)=='D') at line 6026.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (same I2S, no value_profile) blocks in 9/10 trials with avg hitcount=0 and never takes the true side (d=1 L6026 shows L T=0); lacking the partial-match CMP_MAP gradient it cannot incrementally steer the attribute-type token to land on the short 'ID' literal between the longer keyword alternatives.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between value_profile_cmplog and cmplog is value_profile, so the divergence is attributable to the CMP_MAP gradient: value_profile's Hamming/prefix-distance feedback gives intermediate reward as a candidate attribute-type token approaches 'ID', whereas cmplog's edge-only feedback sees the comparison as all-or-nothing and gets no gradient toward the 2-byte literal.

what_input_feature: A DTD element content declaration like '<!ELEMENT a (b,c X' that is structurally deep enough to parse a FIRST element, take a ',' or '|' separator, parse a SECOND child (setting `last` non-NULL at line 6491), then re-enter the separator loop and meet a token that is neither ',' '|' nor ')'.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolved 10/10 with avg hitcount 6 on the true side; its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to the multi-token content-model grammar (DOCTYPE/ELEMENT keyword bytes, '(' , separators) so it climbs to the depth where `last` becomes non-NULL and an invalid trailing token is reached.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolved only 2/10 and blocked 8/10 with avg hitcount 0; lacking the Hamming/prefix-distance CMP_MAP signal it has no gradient toward assembling the second-child structure, so the separator loop never runs the second iteration needed to set `last` and hit the error arm.
mechanism_attribution: The single-technique delta value_profile_cmplog vs cmplog is the CMP_MAP gradient: value-profile buckets give partial-progress credit on the chained byte comparisons that gate the DTD content-model grammar, letting the winner assemble the deep structure that triggers line 6461; cmplog (already having I2S) cannot, so I2S is excluded as the differentiator.

what_input_feature: The input must drive parsing deep into a DTD <!ELEMENT (...) content declaration where xmlParseElementChildrenContentDeclPriv parses a first element, then takes the while-loop body on a ',' or '|' separator to parse a SECOND element, so that both cur and last are non-NULL at line 6513.
why_winner_satisfies: The winner-resolving seed contains a parenthesized content group with element names and an actual separator path: the W overlay hits the '|' branch at 6418 and the second-child parsing at 6484-6508, and the per-role DIVERGENT BRANCHES show W taking the true side of the 6513 blocker (T=6 F=0 and T=3 F=3) where L never does.
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds stall in DTD/external-subset setup (<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...") and either never reach the content loop or exit it after a single element: at 6513 the loser is T=0 F=65, i.e. it always takes the false branch because last stays NULL.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) on top of the I2S that cmplog already has; this gradient rewards partial progress on the long multi-byte literal comparisons (e.g. the #PCDATA CMP7 and DOCTYPE/encoding string compares on the path), letting vpc assemble the structurally deeper multi-child content model that enters the loop a second time, whereas cmplog (I2S only, same as the winner) lacks the gradient and cannot build that depth.

what_input_feature: After a well-formed '<!ELEMENT <name> ' element-declaration prefix, the next three content bytes must be the exact ASCII keyword 'A','N','Y' (0x41 0x4E 0x59) so the chained equality test RAW=='A' && NXT(1)=='N' && NXT(2)=='Y' at parser.c:6728-6729 holds simultaneously.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog reaches and resolves the branch (T=3 on the W side at L6728) and its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose bytes are progressively closer to the three target characters, letting the corpus accrete partial-match seeds (e.g. the 'A'-bearing DTD/keyword bytes visible in the winning seeds) until all three positions land on 'ANY'.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog never takes the true side (loser blocked=10/10, T=0 F=271 at L6728 on the L side): with only edge-bucket + I2S feedback, the three-character keyword has no single intercepted CMP whose operand it can splice, and partial 1-of-3 / 2-of-3 matches earn no new coverage, so the corpus never gets the gradient pressure that finishes the keyword.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) is the sole technique vpc adds over cmplog; it gives partial-match credit toward the multi-byte 'ANY' literal that the edge-only + I2S signal in cmplog cannot reward, so the value-profile feedback is what carries the corpus across the gate.

what_input_feature: Inside an external/internal DTD subset, the input must present a markup-declaration token whose third character after `<` is 'N' (i.e. `<!N...`, a NOTATION declaration), so that switch (NXT(2)) in xmlParseMarkupDecl selects case 'N'. The loser inputs only ever present `<!E`, `<!A`, `<!-` markup tokens (ELEMENT/ATTLIST/comment), never `<!N`.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry a well-formed `<?xml version="1.0"?>` + `<!DOCTYPE` document and, at d=1, take the 'N' switch arm (case 'N': T=6 F=0) reaching the blocker line 6966, while losers stay on case 'E'/'A'. The value_profile_cmplog arm resolved 9/10 trials vs 0/10 for cmplog, and reached the branch 4 times vs 0 (avg hitcount winner=4 loser=0).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds exercise the same DOCTYPE scaffolding but at d=1 land on case 'E' (T=273 F=110) and case 'A' (T=107 F=276) and never on case 'N' (T=0 F=383); xmlParseElementDecl is hit 271 times for L and 0 for W. The loser explores ELEMENT/ATTLIST declarations exhaustively but never synthesizes the `<!N` notation token, so the case 'N' true side stays globally blocked for cmplog.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique difference between value_profile_cmplog (winner) and cmplog (loser) is value_profile, i.e. the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient feedback that value_profile_cmplog adds over cmplog. Both fuzzers already share the same input-to-state comparison-solving mutator, so I2S cannot be the differentiator here. The CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs that get progressively closer (byte-by-byte) to satisfying the NXT(2)=='N' dispatch and the downstream 'NOTATION' keyword comparisons in xmlParseNotationDecl, giving partial-match credit that the pure edge-bucket signal of cmplog never sees, so vpc climbs toward the 'N' arm while cmplog stalls on E/A.

what_input_feature: A DTD-context '%' parameter-entity reference (%name) terminated by a literal ';' character immediately after the parsed name, so that at parser.c:8015 the current char RAW equals ';'. The false branch requires the byte after the PE name to be exactly 0x3b (';').
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the false side 10/10 trials and its winning seeds carry a well-formed PE reference: winning seed 2 contains the byte run '[%.d;' ([ 25 ff 64 3b]) placing a ';' (0x3b) right after a '%'-introduced name, so RAW == ';' holds and the false branch (T=3 F=9 in W) is taken. The CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer to the byte the parser compares against (';'), letting vpc climb toward a terminated PEReference.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the false side 8/10 trials; its blocking seeds reach line 8015 with the true side dominant (L: T=95 F=3), i.e. the byte following the parsed name is not ';'. The cmplog blocking seeds (e.g. '06 00 00 00 31 32 37 37 ... DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1') carry the DOCTYPE/SYSTEM skeleton but no '%name;' whose name is immediately followed by ';', so RAW != ';' stays true and the entity body at 8025+ is never reached.
mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between winner (value_profile_cmplog) and loser (cmplog) is value_profile: the CMP_MAP gradient buckets give partial-credit Hamming/prefix-distance feedback for the RAW vs ';' comparison region, so vpc retains intermediate seeds inching toward the exact ';' terminator. cmplog has the same I2S substitution as vpc but lacks the gradient feedback, so it cannot bank near-miss progress and gets stuck on the true (blocking) side.

what_input_feature: A start-tag attribute whose name is exactly the 5-byte token 'xmlns' (with no namespace prefix), so xmlParseAttribute2 returns attname pointing at the interned ctxt->str_xmlns string and aprefix is NULL. This is a pointer-equality gate against a fixed dictionary literal, requiring the parser to first build a well-formed element with an attribute name byte-equal to 'xmlns'.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the true branch in 8/10 trials with avg hitcount 5; its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that get progressively closer (Hamming/prefix distance) to the 'xmlns' string and to the surrounding well-formed start-tag structure, so partial matches are retained and refined into a literal 'xmlns' attribute name.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves 0/10 trials (blocked all 10, hitcount 0 on the branch). It has I2S but no value-profile gradient, so it gets no graded credit for inputs that approach but do not exactly form the 'xmlns' attribute name; without the gradient it never assembles the exact interned token at the attribute-name position.
mechanism_attribution: The decisive technique delta is value_profile: both fuzzers share I2S (cmplog and vpc), so I2S alone (in the loser) is insufficient. The winner's extra CMP_MAP gradient feedback buckets the closeness of the attribute-name bytes to the 'xmlns' literal, giving intermediate credit that ratchets the corpus toward exact equality at attname == ctxt->str_xmlns.

what_input_feature: An XML document whose <!ELEMENT ...> declaration is processed while ctxt->inSubset==1, i.e. an element decl placed in the internal DTD subset (inline [...] in the DOCTYPE), so xmlSAX2ElementDecl takes the true branch at SAX2.c:787. The loser inputs only ever reach this function with inSubset==2 (external SYSTEM-loaded subset), taking the false (else-if) branch.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning value_profile_cmplog seed prepends an extra '%999/x' + reshaped length-prefix header (offsets 0x00-0x0f differ from every loser) and reaches the true branch: in the per-role overlay line 787 is W-only-true (W T=3 F=0) while lines 788-789 (the internal-subset xmlAddElementDecl with intSubset) are [W] only. value_profile adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that reward partial progress through the chained DOCTYPE/internal-subset structural comparisons, letting vpc assemble an inline internal element declaration that cmplog (edge+I2S only) could not.
why_loser_doesnt: Every cmplog loser seed shares the identical '....127772.xml' filename header and a 'DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' body, an EXTERNAL subset reference. In the overlay the loser only reaches line 790 (else if inSubset==2) and the external-subset arm 791-792/804-805 are [L]; line 787 is L T=0 F=41. cmplog gets zero hits on the true branch (avg hitcount on branch: winner=4 loser=0) and stays blocked 23.90h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets, the value_profile delta that distinguishes vpc from cmplog) gives partial-credit coverage for incrementally matching the multi-byte DOCTYPE / internal-subset structural comparisons, steering the corpus toward an inline internal subset so the inSubset==1 true branch fires; plain cmplog has only edge buckets + I2S substitution and never accumulates that gradient.

what_input_feature: An XML document with a namespaced start element where the element's qualified-name prefix is bound to a matching xmlns declaration, so that inside the namespace loop the interned pointers prefix and pref are identical AND URI is non-NULL (e.g. <p:e xmlns:p="some-uri">), driving line 2332's true side ret->ns = ns.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch 9/10 with hitcount 5 on the true side; the winning seed is a structurally complete XML document beginning <?xml version="1.0"?> with a <!DOCTYPE declaration, the kind of namespaced/DTD-bearing input that reaches xmlNewNs and the prefix==pref identity test. The CMP_MAP gradient feedback that vpc adds over cmplog rewards Hamming/prefix progress on the many string and integer comparisons along the namespace-construction path (dict interning, URI != NULL, the xmlNewNs comparisons), giving partial-credit toward assembling a valid namespaced element.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the branch 10/10 with hitcount 0 on the true side; its winning-shaped seeds keep the false side (T=0 F=33 at L2332) and never set ret->ns at 2333. cmplog has the same edge-bucket feedback as naive plus I2S, but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it gets no partial-credit signal toward the well-formed prefix-binding and stalls at the namespace loop.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets per intercepted CMP) is the single technique vpc has and cmplog lacks. The blocker prefix == pref is a pointer-identity comparison whose operands are runtime-interned heap pointers with no input-byte pre-image, so input-to-state operand substitution (which both fuzzers have) cannot substitute its way across it; only the gradient feedback on the surrounding string/dict comparisons guides the fuzzer to build an input where the prefix interns to the same pointer as a namespace declaration.

what_input_feature: An XML element carrying a namespace prefix bound to a non-NULL URI (so URI != NULL at the blocker) where the namespace-building loop runs (nb_namespaces>0) and the element actually reaches the namespace-resolution stage; the winner inputs build well-formed namespaced markup (e.g. xlink:href attribute / DTD-driven structure) rather than collapsing into the prefix==URI==NULL fast path.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds exercise the namespace loop and namespace-resolution path: at L2321 the loop body runs (W: T=6) and at the blocker L2383 the true side fires (W T=6 F=9). Winner seed bytes contain structured namespace markup such as 'xlink:href' confirming a prefixed namespaced element with a URI.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser (cmplog) seeds never take the blocker true side (L2383 L: T=0 F=161) and the prefix-match assignment at L2332 never fires for the loser (L: T=0), so URI/prefix never align to reach element-namespace resolution; the loser inputs do not converge on the well-formed prefixed-namespace shape.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient on top of cmplog's I2S. Since both fuzzers already have I2S, the differentiator is the CMP_MAP gradient: it gives partial-credit feedback on the byte-by-byte prefix/URI string comparisons (e.g. the prefix == pref pointer/identity and the surrounding namespace-name comparisons) so value_profile_cmplog climbs incrementally toward valid namespaced markup that cmplog's all-or-nothing edge signal cannot bridge.

what_input_feature: A numeric token shaped like 0x<hexdigit>: byte '0' (0x30) at the number start immediately followed by 'x' (0x78) or 'X' (0x58), then a hex digit. The gate requires zNum[1] to equal 'x'/'X' after zNum[0]=='0'.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winning seeds carry literal 0x prefixes inside SELECT expressions, e.g. seed 06e09a37e739e806 'SELECT-0x85E' and seed 239da0d3632856b0 'SELECT -0x11CCC1...'; the byte diff shows W concentrates 0x30('0') at offset 0x08 and 0x78('x') at offset 0x09 exactly where L does not.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser seeds are all-decimal numeric expressions like 'SELECT.5-5/55555' (seed 0c1d027799a90a48) and 'SELECT.5E1-55' (seed 05b666d01ebcac10); they hold 0x35('5') runs at the relevant offsets and never place 'x'/'X' after a '0', so zNum[1]=='x' is always false.
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers in this pair already have the cmplog input-to-state substitution mutator, so that technique is held constant; the only added technique in the winner is value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient. The 'x'/'X' single-byte comparison and the surrounding hex-token compares produce Hamming/prefix-distance buckets that reward seeds whose number byte is creeping toward the 0x form, giving the winner intermediate credit that pure edge feedback (cmplog) cannot see, so it climbs to the literal 0x token.

what_input_feature: A token whose first bytes form the SQL hex-integer prefix: z[0]=='0' AND (z[1]=='x' OR z[1]=='X') AND z[2] is a hex digit, i.e. the literal '0x' (or '0X') immediately followed by [0-9a-fA-F].
why_winner_satisfies: Every value_profile_cmplog winning seed contains an explicit '0x' hex prefix (e.g. 'SELECT-0x85E', 'SELECT -0x11CCC1', 'SELECT -0x111111', 'SELECT -0xC11180'), so z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x', z[2] hex digit all hold and the true (hex-integer) side of line 159595 is taken.
why_loser_doesnt: The cmplog loser-blocking seeds carry decimal digit runs without an 'x'/'X' after a '0' (e.g. '00S', 'SELECT.5-5/55555', 'SELECT.5E1-55'); z[1] is never 'x'/'X', so the (z[1]=='x'||z[1]=='X') sub-condition at 159595:25 evaluates false and the true side is never reached.
mechanism_attribution: Both involved fuzzers carry the same input-to-state substitution mutator; the decisive delta is value_profile. value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward partial Hamming/prefix progress on the chained byte comparisons (z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x'/'X', isxdigit(z[2])), guiding the corpus to assemble the multi-byte '0x' prefix that pure cmplog (edge-bucket-only feedback) cannot reward incrementally.
